steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Label the side pair for the named angle first: opposite, adjacent, and hypotenuse. Then pick the matching shortcut, such as SOH/CAH/TOA, complementary trig values, or a special right-triangle ratio.

steps.title: Locate the angle on the unit circle
steps.content: The angle $$5\pi/6$$ radians lies in the second quadrant because it is between $$\pi/2$$ and $$\pi$$. Its reference angle is $$\pi - 5\pi/6 = \pi/6$$.

steps.title: Compute cosine using reference angle
steps.content: In the second quadrant, cosine is negative. The cosine of the reference angle is $$\cos(\pi/6) = \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}$$. Therefore, $$\cos(5\pi/6) = -\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}$$. That is the correct answer.
